title: Signed minimum slice limits crash the Rust CLI
evidence: Reproduced. apply_python_limit negates i64::MIN -> debug panic; also reachable via --top/--unattributed-libraries. Python exits 0 per spec. Fix: unsigned_abs. Evidence: .goalloop-support/round2.triage.json#G1

title: Rust drops valid scope buckets above i64::MAX
evidence: Reproduced. scopes.rs boundary bucket filter/sort read totals via as_i64().unwrap_or(0); valid u64-range bucket (spec's own example) dropped from output. #G2

title: Occurrence scopes invalidate the aggregate-bound proof and panic Rust
evidence: Reproduced. Occurrence-mode scopes multiply sample values by repeat count; bound proof covers subset sums only -> Python emits 2.7e19 out of contract, Rust panics serializing i128. #G3

title: Rust compare rejects valid u64 report totals
evidence: Reproduced. Rust compare rejects summary total_time_ns in (i64::MAX, u64::MAX] that projections validly emit; Python accepts exactly. #G4

title: Compare still fails open when a row's pct field is missing
evidence: Reproduced. Round-1 strictness kept absent-field->0.0: a PRESENT row missing pct is coerced to 0.0 'improvement' exit 0 in BOTH languages, contradicting spec 'never coerced to zero'. Distinguish row-absent (legit 0) from field-absent (error). #G5

title: Rust regex handling is incompatible and can fail open
evidence: Reproduced. Documented Python-regex vocabulary (incl. lookahead example in docs) rejected by Rust regex crate at pack load; explicit bad regex in target config fails OPEN in Rust (exit 0 all-Other) vs Python exit 2. #G6

title: Signed sample costs disappear from scope rollups
evidence: Reproduced. Both languages drop non-positive buckets/categories/owners; negative aggregates (valid signed values) break additivity (bucket sums 10 vs total 5). Fix: drop only zero, keep negatives, document. #G7

title: Facts-v2 import silently defaults required sample fields
evidence: Reproduced. Missing samples[].values / location_ids / stack silently default to empty in BOTH languages despite spec field contract + validation-error guarantee. #G8

title: Published byte parity fails on valid JSON lexical cases
evidence: Reproduced. Python ensure_ascii escapes unicode, Rust emits raw UTF-8; float exponent 1e-06 vs 1e-6 -> non-facts artifacts not byte-identical, contradicting PARITY.md claim. #G9

title: Rust scope config validation silently substitutes defaults
evidence: Reproduced. Rust accepts empty predicate table and non-string count mode that Python rejects exit 2; silent defaults produce ordinary-looking artifacts. #G10

title: An optional library-regex capture escapes the Python error boundary
evidence: Reproduced. Optional regex capture group participating as None -> Python AttributeError traceback exit 1 (escapes envelope); Rust exits 0 with full-match-derived library. Adopt Rust semantics in Python, document. #G11

title: Rust still omits public runtime-analysis flags
evidence: Reproduced. Rust scopes lacks --fold-runtime-internals/--no-enhanced; Rust targets lacks --verbose-runtime-internals; spec claims full runtime-flag parity. #G12

title: Rust reorders scope cost-kind definitions and changes first-match attribution
evidence: Reproduced. Rust routes scope config through sorted serde_json map, losing cost_kind declaration order; first-match-wins picks ASecond where Python picks ZFirst. #G13

title: Multi-parent target CSV order differs between Python and Rust
evidence: Reproduced. Rust targets outer BTreeMap vs Python first-seen parent order -> csv/simple-csv/text row order diverges for non-alphabetical encounter order. #G14
